knowledge.title: Ancient A site: donut crossfire
knowledge.description: CT A setup: one player donut (close angle behind cover), one player elbow (long angle from connector). Donut traps the entry, elbow trades. Donut alone is exposed to flashes from A main; elbow alone gets killed by the boost. Both is the standard, ~72% A hold rate on full buys.
knowledge.situation: CT-side A defense
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: donut-elbow crossfire is the dominant Ancient A setup

knowledge.title: Ancient B site: cave hold with off-angle
knowledge.description: CT B setup: one player default (behind boxes), one player off-angle in cave entry (the deep corner). Cave off-angle catches the first T through. Avoid the obvious cave hold — Ts pre-fire it 80% of the time.
knowledge.situation: CT-side B defense, anti-rush
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: cave off-angle holds first contact ~60% on Ancient B

knowledge.title: Ancient mid: top mid AWP from CT
knowledge.description: CT AWP top mid holds the entire mid sightline plus a clip into A main. This is the single highest-value AWP angle on Ancient. After the first shot, reposition to elbow — Ts will utility the angle on the second peek.
knowledge.situation: CT AWP positioning
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: top mid AWP averages ~1.4 opening kills per round on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient A execute: smoke ramp + smoke elbow + molly donut
knowledge.description: A execute: smoke ramp (cuts CT rotate), smoke elbow (cuts long angle), molly donut (clears the close hold), flash over from A main. Plant default; lurk from connector. ~70% plant rate with full A execute on Ancient.
knowledge.situation: T-side A execute, full buy
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: full-utility A execute is most reliable T-side strat on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient B execute: smoke A connector + molly cave + flash main
knowledge.description: B execute: smoke A connector (cuts A rotate), molly cave (clears the off-angle hold), flash B main for entry. Plant for ramp angle so post-plant lurker can hold from cave. Ancient B is one of the harder sites to retake when planted correctly.
knowledge.situation: T-side B execute, full buy
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: ramp-side plant outperforms cave-side by ~8 points on Ancient B

knowledge.title: Ancient mid smoke: top mid smoke wins mid duels
knowledge.description: Smoke top mid before any mid push. The top mid smoke takes ~3 seconds to expand fully — throw before you commit, not after. This single smoke neutralizes the AWP and opens A split options.
knowledge.situation: T mid push, AWP top mid
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: top mid smoke is in ~80% of Ancient T mid pushes

knowledge.title: Ancient pistol: CT 2A-1mid-2B with deagle utility
knowledge.description: Ancient pistol favors CT — the close angles reward kevlar and disciplined holds. Standard 2A-1mid-2B with mid player rotating to first contact. Buy P250s + flash + smoke; skip armor only on a stacked rush call.
knowledge.situation: Pistol round, Ancient CT setup
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: CT pistol win rate on Ancient is ~58%, top of active duty

knowledge.title: Ancient force buy: target A through ramp
knowledge.description: Ancient force buys hit A through ramp more reliably than B. Ramp execute creates 2 clean sightlines that force CT trades. B forces on Ancient have low success — too many off-angles to clear with limited utility.
knowledge.situation: T force buy decision on Ancient
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: Ancient ramp force buy wins ~38%, B force wins ~22%

knowledge.title: Ancient AWP retention: cave + tunnel save
knowledge.description: Cave and tunnel give Ancient AWPers safe save routes back to T spawn. Always save the AWP if you have time — Ancient is heavily AWP-dependent on T side, and losing the AWP costs ~20% of next round's win expectation.
knowledge.situation: Ancient save round, AWP retention
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: Ancient AWP retention rate >70% for tier-1 teams

knowledge.title: Ancient elbow duel: pre-aim head level on the gap
knowledge.description: The elbow angle from A connector is a head-level gap. Pre-aim the gap as you peek — this is one of the highest-leverage pre-aim spots on the map. Mis-aimed crosshair = automatic loss to a held angle.
knowledge.situation: Aim duel from elbow
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: top players hold elbow with head-level pre-aim >95% of duels

knowledge.title: Ancient B main: bait smoke before pushing
knowledge.description: B main has a long sightline into cave. Throw a B main smoke (or flash) before any push — the cave off-angle will drop you in your tracks otherwise. This is the most common B-side death cause on Ancient at intermediate skill.
knowledge.situation: T B main push, off-angle deaths
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: B main smoke usage on Ancient correlates with ~25% lower entry death rate

knowledge.title: Ancient close-quarter A: spray > tap on entry
knowledge.description: A site close-quarter fights happen at <5m range — spray, do not tap. The site has so much cover that tap-firing rewards the defender. Spray transfer is the standard kill pattern on Ancient A entries.
knowledge.situation: Close-range A site duel
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: spray-transfer kills account for ~40% of A site frags on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient mid contact: rotate to A on first contact win
knowledge.description: If your mid player wins the first contact in mid, immediately commit to A through ramp. The mid kill takes a CT off the rotate and A becomes 4v3 with full utility. This is Ancient's strongest mid-round trigger.
knowledge.situation: Mid contact win, deciding next call
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: mid-pick into A execute wins ~63% on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient default tempo: slow controls A connector
knowledge.description: Slow defaults on Ancient should pressure A connector for mid information. A connector gives sound cues for both sites and forces a CT response. ~30 second build before the IGL commits is the standard pace.
knowledge.situation: T default round pacing
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tier-1 Ancient T defaults average ~32 seconds before commit

knowledge.title: Ancient anti-strat: stack opposite of last round's hit
knowledge.description: Ancient T sides tend to hit the same site twice in a row when successful. CT IGL: stack opposite of last round's hit on the round AFTER a T win — pro patterns show ~58% repeat rate on Ancient successful executes.
knowledge.situation: CT anti-strat after T site win
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: Ancient T site repeat rate after a win is ~58%

knowledge.title: Ancient A retake: 3-stack from connector with smoke + flash
knowledge.description: A retake from connector with smoke (cuts donut hold), flash, and a 3-man push wins ~50% of full-utility retakes. Avoid main retakes — A main into a planted T is suicide on Ancient.
knowledge.situation: CT retake A site
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: connector retakes on Ancient win ~50%, main retakes ~22%

knowledge.title: Ancient B post-plant: hold from cave deep angle
knowledge.description: After plant on B, lurk back into cave deep — you can pre-fire any CT retake from main and see the rotate from A. Avoid sitting on site — the retake utility from CT spawn punishes static post-plants.
knowledge.situation: T post-plant B, choosing lurk
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: cave-deep post-plant outperforms site-hold by ~15 points on Ancient B

knowledge.title: Ancient A plant: behind temple wall
knowledge.description: Plant the bomb behind the temple wall on A — this exposes the defuser to elbow AND the lurker angle from connector. Avoid plants in the open: the CT can defuse from cover and you cannot pre-fire.
knowledge.situation: T A plant location
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: temple-wall plant has highest post-plant win rate on Ancient A

knowledge.title: Ancient anti-strat: counter A cave push with molly cave exit
knowledge.description: If the T keeps pushing A through cave, molly the cave exit at the 0:40 mark. This is when most cave pushes time their execute. The molly forces a 7-second delay and the CT can re-stack A. Follow with a flash from temple for the counter-peek.
knowledge.situation: CT defending against repeated cave pushes on Ancient
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: timed cave exit molly reduces T A success to ~30%

knowledge.title: Ancient anti-strat: counter B main rush with smoke + crossfire
knowledge.description: If the T rushes B main, smoke the B main exit and play a crossfire behind it — one player site left, one player right. The smoke forces the T to push blind into a crossfire. This is the most effective anti-rush setup on Ancient B.
knowledge.situation: CT defending against B main rushes on Ancient
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: smoke + crossfire B main reduces rush success to ~20%

knowledge.title: Ancient entry: mid push with double utility
knowledge.description: Entry through Ancient mid requires smoke + flash: smoke the CT mid angle, flash over for the peek. Mid on Ancient is the most contested area — dry pushes through mid die to the AWP ~60% of the time. Always use utility.
knowledge.situation: Entry fragger on Ancient mid push
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: utility-assisted mid entry on Ancient succeeds ~50% vs ~25% dry

knowledge.title: Ancient AWP: mid pillar AWP hold
knowledge.description: The CT AWP on Ancient holds mid from the pillar — it covers both T mid approaches and can rotate to either site in 5 seconds. The pillar provides cover for repositioning. This is the default AWP position on Ancient.
knowledge.situation: AWP player on CT Ancient, default position
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: mid pillar AWP on Ancient controls ~65% of T mid approach paths

knowledge.title: Ancient lurk: elbow lurk during A execute
knowledge.description: When the team executes A, the lurker holds elbow or connector. CTs rotating from B through mid pass the lurk position. Ancient has fast rotations — time the ambush for 6-8 seconds after the A execute smokes land.
knowledge.situation: Lurker on T-side Ancient during A execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: elbow lurk catches rotating CTs ~40% of the time on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient support: A retake smoke cave + flash
knowledge.description: Support on Ancient CT should save a smoke and flash for A retakes. Smoke cave (cuts the lurk from T), flash from temple for the retake push. Without the cave smoke, the T lurker gets free kills on retaking CTs.
knowledge.situation: Support player utility for Ancient A retake
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: A retake with cave smoke + flash succeeds ~50% on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient clutch: 1v2 B site — use ramp angle
knowledge.description: In a 1v2 on Ancient B as T post-plant, hold from ramp. The ramp angle watches the defuse from range and has cover from the B main approach. CTs have to clear ramp last because it is the deepest angle on the site.
knowledge.situation: 1v2 clutch on Ancient B as T
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: ramp angle post-plant on Ancient B wins ~25% of 1v2 T clutches

knowledge.title: Ancient: CT cave aggression for info
knowledge.description: Sending one CT aggressively into cave early can catch the T setup off guard. If you spot 3+ Ts in cave, call the A stack and rotate the B player. Cave info is the highest-value early-round read on Ancient.
knowledge.situation: CT Ancient, need info on T setup
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: early CT cave aggression provides useful info ~55% of rounds on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient: B retake from site with smoke B main
knowledge.description: CT retaking B must smoke B main to cut the T reinforcement path. Push from site back into the plant area with a flash. Without the B main smoke, T players cycle through B main for free kills on retaking CTs.
knowledge.situation: CT retake B site on Ancient
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: B retake with B main smoke succeeds ~45% vs ~20% without on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient IGL: cave read timing for B commit
knowledge.description: At the 0:20 mark, listen for cave footsteps and utility. Heavy cave activity at 0:20 signals a fast B hit — rotate a third player to B immediately. If cave is silent until 0:40, the T is likely defaulting A or mid. Base your stack on the cave timing, not the mid contact.
knowledge.situation: CT-side Ancient, IGL reading cave timing for B defense
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: cave audio timing correctly predicts B commits in ~58% of T defaults on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient support: B retake flash from CT spawn
knowledge.description: Save one flash for B retakes. Throw a CT-spawn pop flash that bounces off the back wall and blinds anyone on site facing ramp or cave. Push from CT spawn immediately after the flash pops. This single flash raises B retake success by ~10 points.
knowledge.situation: Support player holding utility for B retake on Ancient
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: CT spawn flash B retake succeeds ~42% vs ~28% dry on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient clutch: 1v1 A site — use temple wall angle
knowledge.description: In a 1v1 on Ancient A as T post-plant, hold from behind the temple wall. The temple wall gives you sight on the bomb and cover from both connector and main approaches. CTs must commit to the open site to defuse — you have the first shot from cover.
knowledge.situation: 1v1 clutch on Ancient A site as T
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: temple wall post-plant on Ancient A wins ~55% of 1v1 T clutches

knowledge.title: Ancient AWP: mid elbow angle from CT
knowledge.description: Hold the mid elbow angle from the CT side with the AWP. The elbow creates a narrow sightline that catches T mid pushes before they reach A connector. After the first kill, reposition to donut — the T will flash or smoke the elbow on their next attempt.
knowledge.situation: CT AWP holding mid elbow on Ancient
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: mid elbow AWP averages 0.9 opening kills per half on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient anti-strat: counter A split with donut aggression
knowledge.description: If the T keeps splitting A from main and connector simultaneously, push one player aggressively to donut at the 0:15 mark. The donut player contests the connector approach and disrupts the split timing. Fall back to site after one kill or after gathering info on the split direction.
knowledge.situation: CT losing A to repeated splits on Ancient
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: donut aggression disrupts A splits ~48% of the time on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient entry: B through main with pre-flash
knowledge.description: Entry through B main requires a pop flash that bounces off the right wall and blinds the cave off-angle. Push immediately after the flash — the cave player is blind for 1.5 seconds. Without the flash, the cave off-angle kills entries ~55% of the time on Ancient B.
knowledge.situation: Entry fragger pushing B main on Ancient
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: pop flash B main entry succeeds ~50% vs ~28% dry on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient lurk: cave lurk during A execute
knowledge.description: When the team executes A, lurk in cave entrance. CTs rotating from B through mid pass the cave sightline. Time the ambush for 7-9 seconds after the A execute smokes land. The cave lurk on Ancient catches B rotators from an unexpected angle.
knowledge.situation: Lurker on T-side Ancient during A execute, cave position
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: cave lurk catches B-to-A rotators ~35% of the time on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient economy: force buy mid push with Galils
knowledge.description: On force buy rounds, push mid aggressively with armor + Galils. Ancient mid favors the aggressor — the angles are close enough for Galil spray and the mid control opens both sites. Avoid force-buying into A main or B main where the CT AWP dominates.
knowledge.situation: T force buy decision on Ancient
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: mid force push with Galils wins ~36% on Ancient — highest force buy WR for the map

knowledge.title: Ancient positioning: A donut defense with off-angle
knowledge.description: Hold A donut from the deep off-angle behind the pillar, not the obvious donut top. The deep angle catches T entries as they clear the standard donut position and look away. Fall back to site after one kill — staying donut after contact exposes you to the trade.
knowledge.situation: CT A donut positioning on Ancient
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: deep donut off-angle wins first contact ~58% on Ancient A

knowledge.title: Ancient B retake: ramp push with cave smoke
knowledge.description: Retake B from ramp with a smoke on cave to isolate the T lurker. Push from ramp toward site with a flash from CT spawn. The ramp approach avoids the B main post-plant angles. Without the cave smoke, T players in cave get free kills on retaking CTs.
knowledge.situation: CT retaking B from ramp on Ancient
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: ramp retake with cave smoke succeeds ~44% on Ancient B

knowledge.title: Ancient utility: cave smoke for safe B approach
knowledge.description: Throw a cave smoke from T spawn that blocks the cave defender sightline. This single smoke enables safe passage through cave to B site. Without the smoke, the cave off-angle picks T entries in ~40% of B pushes. The cave smoke is the most cost-effective T utility on Ancient.
knowledge.situation: T-side Ancient, safe cave passage
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: cave smoke reduces T entry deaths from ~40% to ~10% on Ancient B approaches

knowledge.title: Ancient mid-round: mid control after donut trade
knowledge.description: After winning a donut trade, take mid control within 5 seconds. Mid control from donut opens both A ramp and B cave options. Do not slow play after the donut trade — the value decays as the CT repositions. Push mid, clear elbow, and hold for the IGL commit call.
knowledge.situation: T-side Ancient, post-donut-trade mid control
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: immediate mid push after donut trade raises round win rate by ~12 points on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient IGL: second-half T adjustment read
knowledge.description: When switching to T side on Ancient, review CT defense patterns from the first half. If the enemy played aggressive cave pushes, open with a fast A execute through main to punish the undermanned A defense. If they stacked A with three players, default B through cave and exploit the slow CT rotation. Commit to the adjustment in round 16 before the CTs settle.
knowledge.situation: T-side Ancient, second-half opening rounds as IGL
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: IGLs who exploit first-half CT tendencies win ~9% more opening rounds after half-switch on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient IGL: timeout trigger on donut control loss
knowledge.description: Call a timeout on Ancient after losing two consecutive rounds where the Ts took donut control early. Donut is the mid pivot of Ancient — losing it opens both A and B simultaneously. Use the timeout to assign a dedicated donut AWP, set a pre-molotov on the donut push lane, and adjust the mid player to support the donut hold with a flash.
knowledge.situation: CT-side Ancient, losing donut control repeatedly
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: timeouts after losing donut control recover the setup in ~54% of cases on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient lurk: cave to B ramp flank route
knowledge.description: As the T lurker on Ancient, push through cave and position on B ramp while your team executes A main. Wait until you hear the A execute utility, then push B ramp aggressively. The B anchor will rotate toward A on the execute call — you catch them mid-rotation or take B site uncontested. Walk through cave to avoid giving away the flank.
knowledge.situation: T-side Ancient, lurk during A main execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: cave lurk flanks during A executes produce a kill in ~34% of rounds on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient lurk: CT elbow flank during B retake
knowledge.description: As the CT lurker on Ancient, hold elbow during a B site retake. When your team pushes B through the ramp entrance, push elbow to cut off the T escape route through cave. Time your push to arrive 3 seconds after the B retake begins — Ts rotating to save through cave will run into your crosshair at the elbow corner.
knowledge.situation: CT-side Ancient, flanking during B retake
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: elbow flanks during B retakes catch rotating Ts ~31% of the time on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient clutch: 1v1 CT defuse on A site from temple pillar
knowledge.description: In a 1v1 CT defuse situation on Ancient A, use the temple pillar as your anchor. Tap the defuse to force the T to peek from main or donut, then hold the tight angle around the pillar. Temple pillar provides cover from both common post-plant spots. If the T does not peek within 5 seconds, commit to the full defuse with a smoke on the bomb.
knowledge.situation: 1v1 CT clutch on Ancient A site
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: temple pillar 1v1 defuse attempts succeed ~41% of the time on Ancient A

knowledge.title: Ancient clutch: 1v3 T bomb timer play on B site
knowledge.description: In a 1v3 T post-plant on Ancient B, plant for cave and retreat into the cave entrance. From cave you control the only bomb line of sight without exposing yourself to multiple angles. Molotov the bomb at 10 seconds to deny the defuse. The three CTs must push through the narrow ramp or rotate through mid — both paths funnel them into your angle.
knowledge.situation: 1v3 T post-plant clutch on Ancient B site
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: cave post-plant with B plant wins ~20% of 1v3 situations on Ancient B

knowledge.title: Ancient aim: A main to temple duel spot
knowledge.description: Hold the A main entrance from the temple head-glitch position. This gives a long-range head-level angle on Ts pushing through A main. Pre-aim the center of the doorway where Ts emerge — they must swing wide into your crosshair to clear the site. Counter-strafe and tap at this range. The temple head-glitch exposes only your forehead and trades favorably against aggressive A pushes.
knowledge.situation: CT-side Ancient, holding A main from temple
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: temple head-glitch wins the A main duel ~56% of the time at pro level on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient aim: mid wallbang through the stone gap
knowledge.description: The stone barrier at mid on Ancient has a thin gap that allows wallbang shots with AK-47. From the T side, pre-aim the gap at head height and fire 2-3 rounds when you hear CT movement behind the barrier. The penetration deals ~30% rifle damage. This wallbang is especially effective at round start when CTs cross mid to take donut control — timing it with the crossing window maximizes hit probability.
knowledge.situation: T-side Ancient, mid barrier wallbang
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: mid barrier wallbangs score damage in ~15% of rounds where CTs contest mid on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient support: A execute triple-utility combo
knowledge.description: As the support player during an Ancient A execute, throw the CT smoke (blocking CT spawn rotation), the donut molotov (clearing the donut anchor), and the temple flash (blinding the site player). Execute in this order: smoke first to cut off rotations, molotov second to flush the donut angle, flash last to enable the entry fragger. This 7-second combo isolates the A site to one or zero defenders.
knowledge.situation: T-side Ancient, supporting A site execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: full CT-donut-temple utility combo raises A execute success to ~59% on Ancient

knowledge.title: Ancient entry: alternate B entry through cave shortcut
knowledge.description: Instead of the standard B ramp push, enter B site through the cave shortcut with a right-hand pop flash. The cave path bypasses the ramp crossfire and puts you on B site from behind the pillar. Flash off the cave wall so it pops above the site — this blinds the ramp and platform defenders. Swing immediately after the flash to catch the B anchor repositioning.
knowledge.situation: T-side Ancient, alternate B entry through cave
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: cave shortcut entries score first kill ~43% of the time on Ancient B

knowledge.title: Ancient economy: A main force-buy with Deagles and armor
knowledge.description: On Ancient eco rounds as T, buy three Desert Eagles with kevlar for an A main push. The A main corridor on Ancient has medium-range engagements that suit the Deagle one-shot headshot range. Stack all three at A main entrance and peek sequentially — the first Deagle trades with the A anchor, the second and third clean up. Kevlar ensures you survive the first body shot exchange.
knowledge.situation: T-side Ancient, eco round A main push
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: A main Deagle pushes on eco rounds average 1.5 kills and break CT economy ~23% of the time on Ancient
